title: achillea oxyloba subsp. oxyloba
intro: Achillea oxyloba subsp. oxyloba, also known as the oxyloba yarrow, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family, native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, containing compounds such as flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Achillea oxyloba subsp. oxyloba include reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to soothe menstrual cramps, calm digestive issues, and promote overall well-being.
